title: SSC CHSL 2026
meta_description: SSC CHSL 2026 — 12th pass government jobs as LDC, Postal Assistant, DEO. Exam pattern, syllabus, salary ₹20-35K/month, eligibility, and preparation strategy.

hero.one_liner: Government jobs for 12th pass candidates — LDC, Postal Assistant, Data Entry Operator, and Sorting Assistant in central government departments

what_is_it: SSC CHSL (Combined Higher Secondary Level) is an annual exam conducted by the Staff Selection Commission for recruiting 12th pass (10+2) candidates into Group C posts in central government ministries and departments. It's one of the most accessible government exams since it requires only 12th pass qualification — no graduation needed.

The major posts recruited through CHSL are: Lower Division Clerk (LDC) / Junior Secretariat Assistant (JSA) — clerical work in government offices, Postal Assistant (PA) / Sorting Assistant (SA) — work in post offices and mail sorting centers, Data Entry Operator (DEO) — data entry in government offices, and DEO Grade A — data entry in offices under the Comptroller & Auditor General (CAG).

The exam has a two-tier structure: Tier 1 (Computer Based Test — screening) and Tier 2 (Computer Based Test — merit + Skill Test for typing/data entry speed). The Tier 1 pattern is very similar to SSC CGL Tier 1 — same subjects, same duration, same number of questions — making it possible to prepare for both simultaneously.
For 2026, the SSC Calendar indicates CHSL notification in April and Tier 1 exam in July-September 2026. Expected vacancies are approximately 6,000 posts across all categories.

key_details.label: Education
key_details.value: 12th pass (10+2) or equivalent from a recognized board

key_details.label: Selection Process
key_details.value: Tier 1 (CBT, qualifying) → Tier 2 (CBT, merit + Skill Test for typing/DEO)

key_details.label: Exam Language
key_details.value: English and Hindi (bilingual) except English Comprehension section

exam_pattern.description: Same pattern as SSC CGL Tier 1. This is purely qualifying — marks don't count in final merit. 100 MCQs across 4 sections.

Tier 2 determines your final rank. It includes objective sections (like CGL Tier 2 but easier) plus a typing/skill test that is qualifying in nature. For LDC/JSA: English typing 35 wpm or Hindi typing 30 wpm. For DEO: Data entry speed of 8,000 key depressions per hour.

extra_sections.heading: CHSL vs CGL — Which Should You Take?
content: If you have a graduation degree, you should appear for BOTH CGL and CHSL simultaneously — the Tier 1 syllabus is identical. CGL offers higher posts (Level 4-8, salary ₹25,000-60,000) while CHSL offers Level 2-5 posts (₹22,000-35,000). Many candidates use CHSL as a safety net while aiming for CGL.

If you only have 12th pass qualification, CHSL is your primary exam. You can appear for CGL after completing graduation. CHSL posts offer genuine career growth — LDCs can be promoted to UDC, then Assistant through departmental exams.

Key difference: CGL has more competitive cutoffs and harder Tier 2 papers. CHSL is relatively easier to crack with 4-5 months of dedicated preparation. Many first-time aspirants secure government jobs through CHSL while continuing to prepare for CGL.

preparation_tips: Since Tier 1 is identical to CGL Tier 1, use the same preparation resources. Focus on NCERT 6-10 for GK, RS Aggarwal for Reasoning, Kiran's Math for Quant, and Wren & Martin for English grammar.

Practice typing daily from month 3 onwards. For LDC post, you need 35 wpm in English or 30 wpm in Hindi. Use free typing tutor websites. Start with accuracy, then build speed. 30 minutes of typing practice daily for 3 months is sufficient.
Previous year papers are the best resource. SSC CHSL repeats question patterns heavily. Solve at least 15-20 previous year Tier 1 papers before the exam.
For DEO post, practice data entry speed. You need 8,000 key depressions per hour — this is about 133 characters per minute, which is achievable with regular typing practice.

faqs.question: Can graduates apply for CHSL?
faqs.answer: Yes. Anyone who has passed 12th or higher can apply. Many graduates and even post-graduates appear for CHSL as a backup while preparing for higher exams like CGL or banking.

faqs.question: Is there an interview in CHSL?
faqs.answer: No. Selection is purely based on Tier 1 + Tier 2 + Skill Test (typing/DEO). There is no interview or personality test. This makes CHSL more objective and less subjective than exams with interviews.

faqs.question: What is the typing test like?
faqs.answer: For LDC/JSA: You're given a passage to type on a computer. Speed required: 35 wpm in English or 30 wpm in Hindi (with approximately 5% error allowance). Duration: 15 minutes. This is qualifying — you either pass or fail. For DEO: Data entry from a given format at 8,000 key depressions per hour for 15 minutes.

faqs.question: Postal Assistant — is it a good post?
faqs.answer: Yes. Postal Assistant is at Pay Level 4 (higher than LDC's Level 2), giving ₹25,000-32,000/month starting salary. Work involves counter operations, savings schemes, money orders, and postal services. Promotions to Inspector Posts and above are possible. Posting is usually in your home state/region.

faqs.question: How many attempts are allowed?
faqs.answer: Unlimited, as long as you meet the age criteria (27 for General, with relaxations for reserved categories). You can keep appearing every year until you reach the upper age limit.
